text: Here we present a case of a sixty-year-old patient with symptomatic grade IV hemorrhoids who underwent closed hemorrhoidectomy.
The patient is positioned in the prone jack-knife position, lying face down with hips elevated and knees bent, facilitating access to the anal region for the surgical procedure.
The surgeon performs a dorsal perineal block by injecting a mixture of lidocaine and bupivacaine into three different directions at each injection site around the anal region.
The surgeon inserts an Onsler anoscope into the anal canal, providing a clear view of the surgical site for the hemorrhoidectomy procedure.
The surgeon uses forceps to retract the hemorrhoid located at the three o'clock position into the lumen, followed by a V-shaped excision extending up to two centimeters above the dentate line.
The surgeon applies continuous sutures to ligate the vascular pedicle, extending the sutures up to the perineal skin.
The surgeon repeats the V-shaped excision at the eleven o'clock position, retracting the pile into the lumen with forceps and excising up to two centimeters above the dentate line.
The surgeon performs coagulation and suture pexy at the seven o'clock position, securing the tissue with sutures.
The surgeon places a hemostatic sponge intra-anally, visible within the surgical field through the Onsler anoscope.
